content: We can reframe parallel orchestration and ingestion systems with reader agents as a biological pathogen-immune interaction network, where each reader agent acts as a specialized biological agent that exerts power to trigger specific processing effects on ingested data streams. Just as multiple distinct biological agents can simultaneously interact with different host tissues to produce coordinated systemic responses, multiple reader agents can operate in parallel to independently ingest and process disjoint data partitions to speed up overall pipeline throughput.
source_topics: Parallel Orchestration & Ingestion with Reader Agents
trigger_topic: Parallel Orchestration & Ingestion with Reader Agents
